title: variant and type-safe alternatives deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Added digest-pinned deterministic executable evidence for variant and type-safe alternatives; AI-assisted draft remains human-unreviewed and exam-ineligible.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify language-version accuracy, distractor validity, equivalence, and grading evidence before formal use.
items.title: variant and type-safe alternatives: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this CPP misconception? std::get automatically converts whichever variant alternative is active into the requested type.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. A variant has one active declared alternative; check or visit it, because std::get for the wrong alternative throws bad_variant_access.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. A variant has one active declared alternative; check or visit it, because std::get for the wrong alternative throws bad_variant_access.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned CPP correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: A variant has one active declared alternative; check or visit it, because std::get for the wrong alternative throws bad_variant_access.
std::get automatically converts whichever variant alternative is active into the requested type.

answer.explanation: A variant has one active declared alternative; check or visit it, because std::get for the wrong alternative throws bad_variant_access.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: A human reviewer must verify language-version accuracy, distractor validity, equivalence, and grading evidence before formal use.
items.title: variant and type-safe alternatives: complete the language rule
items.prompt: Complete the missing CPP checkpoint in the source-aligned correction. Use the exact token or phrase required by this draft.
hints: Use the checkpoint that distinguishes the valid rule from this misconception: std::get automatically converts whichever variant alternative is active into the requested type.

feedback.correct: Correct. The required checkpoint is 'active declared alternative'.
feedback.incorrect: Recheck the exact language rule and its boundary. A variant has one active declared alternative; check or visit it, because std::get for the wrong alternative throws bad_variant_access.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact CPP token or phrase that completes the reviewed rule candidate.

items.template: A variant has one {{key-rule}}; check or visit it, because std::get for the wrong alternative throws bad_variant_access.

answer.explanation: A variant has one active declared alternative; check or visit it, because std::get for the wrong alternative throws bad_variant_access.

items.title: variant and type-safe alternatives: executable application
items.prompt: Read tag i or s and a matching integer or word into std::variant<int,std::string>, then use std::visit to print int:value or text:value.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
Reference verification uses the digest-pinned local container with network=none, no package installation, a read-only source mount, and deterministic stdin/stdout.
Executable facet: closed type-safe sum type and exhaustive visitor

answer.explanation: The reference solution exercises closed type-safe sum type and exhaustive visitor under the bounded deterministic contract; it remains an AI-assisted draft pending human review.
